item.title: 构建基于 Web 的 AI 应用程序的最佳堆栈是什么？ | What's the best stack for building web based AI applications?
item.description:
具有免费套餐的产品是首选。

原文:

   

OpenAI, chatGPT, DALL-E have excellent APIs but what are the best tools for building SaaS products based on them and offering services to end users? Products with a free tier are preferred.

Adam Mateusz Brożyński - Jan 29

Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - Mar 2

item.title: #DEVDiscuss：人工智能的未来 | #DEVDiscuss: The Future of AI
item.description:

问题：

  • 关于这个主题，您想分享任何成功、失败或其他故事吗？

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 devteam 的操作：

image created by Margaux Peltat for the Chilled Cow YouTube channel

Time for #DEVDiscuss — right here on DEV 😎

Inspired by @brownrita460's Top 7 post, tonight’s topic is...the future of AI 🤖🔮

Questions:

  • How do you think the proliferation of AI will affect society over the next 10 years? How about 20 years? 100 years?
  • How does this topic make you feel? Scared, excited, optimistic, cynical, or skeptical? Or something else?
  • Any triumphs, fails, or other stories you'd like to share on this topic?

item.author: erinposting

item.title: 使用 API 网关高效管理您的 GraphQL API | Efficiently Manage Your GraphQL API with API Gateway
item.description:
这使得它比传统的REST API更加高效和灵活，后者通常需要多个请求来检索相同的数据。 但是，管理 GraphQL API 可能既复杂又耗时，尤其是在大规模情况下。 这就是API 网关的用武之地。

现代 API 网关（例如Apache APISIX）的关键特性之一是它对 GraphQL API 的支持。 一个这样的插件是degrapghql插件，它允许我们将 GraphQL API 转换为 REST API。 在这篇文章中，我们将通过一个例子来探索这个特性。

学习目标

您将在整篇文章中学习并找到以下问题的答案：

  • 什么是 DeGraphQL 插件？
  • 如何使用 DeGraphQL 插件。
  • 如何将 REST 转换为 GraphQL？
  • 如何管理 GraphQL API 流量？

为什么要使用 DeGraphQL 插件？

该插件能够通过将 URI 映射到GraphQL 查询，将 GraphQL 上游（后端服务）公开的 API 转换为传统的 REST 端点。 从典型客户端调用 REST API 为更多人带来 GraphQL 的好处，请考虑以下用例：

用例 1：您的现有客户习惯使用 REST API，不太熟悉如何编写 GraphQL 查询。 为了让事情变得简单，您可以使用 Apache APISIX API Gateway 将 GraphQL API 转换为 REST API。

用例 2：您在前端开发团队中，希望通过 REST API 尝试现有的 GraphQL API 功能，而不要求后端团队实现新的 GraphQL 服务器。

用例 3：您无权更改后端，因为它是一组现有的 GraphQL API，可能由第 3 方管理。

用例 4：您拥有现有的 REST API 基础设施，但您希望评估 GraphQL 是否可以满足您的需求。

用例 5：您有一个大型代码库，并且 GraphQL 迁移正在后端进行，但您希望立即使用 GraphQL 而无需等待。

用例 6：您有多个微服务，它们结合使用了这两种方法。 您希望在它们之间实现顺畅的通信。

请求验证：它可以验证传入的 GraphQL 请求以确保它们满足特定条件。 这可以包括检查查询的结构、实施输入类型约束等。 通过验证请求，您可以确保您的 API 始终接收有效且格式正确的请求。

查询解析：它还可以解析 GraphQL 查询，允许您从查询中提取特定信息并使用它来通知 API 的行为。 这可能包括诸如根据请求的数据选择适当的后端服务，或根据某些查询参数修改响应之类的事情。

响应转换：最后，它可以在将 GraphQL 响应返回给客户端之前对其进行转换。 这对于规范化数据结构、删除敏感信息或向响应添加额外数据等事情很有用。

有了这个功能，Apache APISIX不仅可以轻松地将 REST 和 GraphQL 一起使用，而且您还可以定义速率限制、强制执行身份验证和授权、阻止试图滥用 API 的客户端，并确保 API 在帮助更新时无缝工作其他内置插件。

如何使用 DeGraphQL 插件 (Demo)

有了足够的理论知识，现在我们可以进入DeGraphQL 插件的实际演示。 例如，我们将使用一个免费的公共 GraphQL API 来检索有关国家、大陆和语言的信息https://countries.trevorblades.com/ 。

如果您导航到上面的 Countries API 链接，它将打开一个游乐场，您可以在其中针对 UI 上的 GraphQL API 编写一些查询。

您还可以使用StepZen或ApollographQL提供的 GraphQL 工作室构建您自己的 GraphQL API，这些工作室通过组合预构建的 API（例如 Accuweather、Airtable、GitHub、Twitter、Trello 等）帮助您构建和部署您自己的 GraphQL API。 例如，您将两个 Accuteweather 和 Countries API 组合在一起以收集国家/城市名称提供的天气信息，并将 APISIX 放在前面以从 REST 查询 API。

现在我们的任务是将上述查询定义转换为简单的 REST 调用并将其作为 JSON 数据发送。 因此，Apache APISIX API 网关公开了 REST 端点，并且应该能够将所有请求路由到 GraphQL API。

例如，所有对 API 网关/country-info URI 路径的 REST 请求都应转换并传递给 GraphQL countries API https://countries.trevorblades.com/graphql 。

一个示例 curl 命令，看起来像这样：

 curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/country-info -X POST -d \ '{ "code": "EE" }'

我们应该从 API 得到如下响应：

 { "data": { "country": { "code": "EE", "capital": "Tallinn", "currency": "EUR", "languages": [ { "name": "Estonian" } ] } } }

在接下来的部分中，我们将学习如何逐步实现这一目标。

让我们启动并运行 Apache APISIX

在使用 degrapghql 插件之前，您需要安装 Apache APISIX。 您可以按照 Apache APISIX 网站上的安装说明开始安装。

先决条件

  • 您还可以使用Postman等简单工具与 API 进行交互。

这将创建两个 Docker 容器——一个用于存储配置的 etcd 和 APISIX 网关本身。 一旦您看到“✔ APISIX 已准备就绪！” 消息，我们可以通过 APISIX 的管理 API配置上游、插件和路由，代理对 GraphQL API 的请求。

创建上游

接下来，我们将创建一个上游对象以在 API 网关中注册我们的Countries GrapghQL API ：

 curl "http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/upstreams/1" -X PUT -d ' { "name": "GraphQL API upstream", "desc": "Register Countries GraphQL API as the upstream", "type": "roundrobin", "scheme": "https", "nodes": { "countries.trevorblades.com": 1 } }'

创建插件配置

接下来，设置新的插件配置对象。 我们将使用两个转换插件proxy-rewrite和degraphql来重写请求的主机和 URI，并分别向 GraphQL API 进行查询。

 curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/plugin_configs/1 -X PUT -d ' { "plugins":{ "proxy-rewrite":{ "uri":"/graphql", "host":"countries.trevorblades.com" }, "degraphql":{ "query":"query Country($code: ID!) { country(code: $code) { code capital currency languages { name } } }", "variables":[ "code" ] } } }'

在上面的 DeGraphQL 插件配置中，我们设置了query和variables两个属性。 可以在 REST 调用中的 Post 请求正文或 URI 上定义 GraphQL 查询变量。

在这种情况下，我们正在执行的查询如下所示，您可以将其替换为您自己的查询：

 query ($code: ID!) { country(code: $code) { code capital currency languages { name } } }

使用 DeGraphQL 插件创建路由

此步骤涉及设置一个使用插件配置的新路由，以及配置路由以与我们在前面的步骤中创建的上游（通过引用它们的 ID）一起工作：

 curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/1 -X PUT -d ' { "name":"GraphQL API route", "desc":"Create a new route in APISIX for the Countries GraphQL API", "uri":"/country-info", "upstream_id":"1", "plugin_config_id":1 }'

测试 DeGraphQL 插件激活

现在让我们使用以下 curl 命令测试这个新设置。

 curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/country-info -X POST -d \ '{ "code": "EE" }'

我们将从 APISIX 得到响应：

 { "data": { "country": { "code": "EE", "capital": "Tallinn", "currency": "EUR", "languages": [ { "name": "Estonian" } ] } } }

同样的code变量也可以作为 GET 参数提供：

 curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/country-info?code=EE

响应转换

借助 Apisix 的响应重写插件，可以在将 GraphQL 响应返回给客户端之前对其进行转换。 让我们使用此插件从响应 JSON 中删除currency键和值以仅显示其他所有内容。 为此，我们需要将response-rewrite插件添加到现有的插件配置中。

 curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/plugin_configs/1 -X PATCH -d ' { "plugins":{ "response-rewrite": { "filters":[ { "regex":"(?:\"currency\":\")(.*?)(?:\")", "scope":"once", "replace":"" } ], "vars":[ [ "status", "==", 200 ] ] } } }'

安装此插件后，您可以再次向/country-info发出请求并查看转换后的响应：

 { "data": { "country": { "code": "EE", "capital": "Tallinn", "languages": [ { "name": "Estonian" } ] } } }

概括

总的来说，DeGraphQL 插件是任何使用 APISIX 构建 GraphQL API 的开发人员的必备工具。 它具有强大的功能和易于使用的配置，可以轻而易举地集成到您现有的 API 网关中，同时它支持 GraphQL 特定的功能，确保您的 API 具有高性能、可靠和可扩展性。

相关资源

  • 什么是 GraphQL？ 推荐内容

    社区

    关于作者

    访问我的个人博客： www.iambobur.com



原文:

GraphQL is a powerful query language for APIs that allows developers to define the structure of the data they need from the server, and the server responds with only that data. This makes it much more efficient and flexible than traditional REST APIs, which often require multiple requests to retrieve the same data. However, managing GraphQL APIs can be complex and time-consuming, especially at scale. This is where an API Gateway comes in.

One of the key features of modern API Gateways such as Apache APISIX is its support for GraphQL APIs. APISIX makes it easy to manage and scale GraphQL APIs using its flexible configuration system and powerful plugins. One such plugin is the degrapghql plugin, which allows us to convert the GraphQL API into a REST API. In this post, we will explore this feature with an example.

Learning objectives

You will learn and find out answers to the following questions throughout the article:

  • What is the DeGraphQL plugin?
  • What are the use cases and features of the DeGraphQL plugin?
  • How to use the DeGraphQL plugin.
  • How to transform REST to GraphQL?
  • How to manage GraphQL API traffic?

Why use DeGraphQL plugin?

This plugin is able to transform APIs exposed by a GraphQL upstream (backend service) into a traditional REST endpoint by mapping URIs into GraphQL queries. Calling REST APIs from a typical client opens the benefits of GraphQL for more people, consider the following use-cases:

Use-case 1: Your existing customers are used to consuming REST APIs and are not too familiar with how to write GraphQL queries. In order to keep things simple for them, you can use Apache APISIX API Gateway to convert GraphQL API to a REST API.

Use-case 2: You are in a front-end development team that wants to try existing GraphQL API functionalities through REST API without asking for the back-end team to implement a new GraphQL server.

Use-case 3: You have no access to change the backend because it's an existing set of GraphQL APIs, potentially managed by a 3rd party.

Use-case 4: You have an existing REST API infrastructure, but you're looking to evaluate whether GraphQL can work for your needs.

Use-case 5: You have a large codebase, and the GraphQL migration is happening on the backend, but you want to use GraphQL now without waiting.

Use-case 6: You have multiple microservices and they use the combination of both approaches. You want to enable smooth communication between them.

What are the DeGraphQL plugin features?

DeGraphQL plugin provides a number of useful features that make it easy to configure and manage your GraphQL API, including:

Request Validation: It can validate incoming GraphQL requests to ensure they meet certain criteria. This can include checking the structure of the query, enforcing input type constraints, and more. By validating requests, you can ensure that your API is always receiving valid and well-formed requests.

Query Parsing: It can also parse GraphQL queries, allowing you to extract specific information from the query and use it to inform your API's behavior. This can include things like selecting the appropriate backend service based on the requested data, or modifying the response based on certain query parameters.

Response Transformation: Finally, it can transform GraphQL responses before they are returned to the client. This can be useful for things like normalizing data structures, removing sensitive information, or adding additional data to the response.

With this capability, Apache APISIX not only makes it easy to use REST and GraphQL together but you can also define rate limits, enforce authentication and authorization, block clients that attempt to misuse an API, and ensure APIs work seamlessly as they are updated with help of other built-in plugins.

How to use DeGraphQL plugin (Demo)

With enough theoretical knowledge in mind, now we can jump into a practical demo of DeGraphQL plugin. DeGraphQL needs a GraphQL endpoint to query. As an example, we are going to use one of the free public GraphQL APIs that retrieve information about countries, continents, and languages https://countries.trevorblades.com/.

If you navigate to the above Countries API link, it will open a playground where you can write some queries against GraphQL API on the UI.

You can also build your own GraphQL API using StepZen or ApollographQL provided GraphQL studios that helps you build and deploy your own GraphQL API by combining prebuilt APIs such as Accuweather, Airtable, GitHub, Twitter, Trello, and more. For example, you compose two Accuteweather and Countries APIs together to collect the weather information provided by a country/city name and put APISIX at the front to query the API from REST.

Now our task is to transform the above query definition to a simple REST call and send it as JSON data. As an outcome, the Apache APISIX API Gateway exposes the REST endpoint and should be able to route all requests to the GraphQL API.

For example, all REST requests to API Gateway /country-info URI path with an underlining query by a country code should be converted and passed to GraphQL countries API https://countries.trevorblades.com/graphql.

An example curl command that looks something like this:

curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/country-info -X POST -d \
'{ "code": "EE"
}'

And we should get a response from the API as follows:

{ "data": { "country": { "code": "EE", "capital": "Tallinn", "currency": "EUR", "languages": [ { "name": "Estonian" } ] } }
}

In the next sections, we will learn how to achieve this step-by-step.

Let’s get Apache APISIX up and running

Before you can use the degrapghql plugin, you'll need to install Apache APISIX. You can follow the installation instructions on the Apache APISIX website to get started.

Prerequisites

  • Docker is used to installing the containerized etcd and APISIX.
  • curl is used to send requests to APISIX for validation. You can also use easy tools such as Postman to interact with the API.

APISIX can be easily installed and started with the following quickstart script:

curl -sL https://run.api7.ai/apisix/quickstart | sh

This will create two Docker containers – a etcd to store configuration and APISIX Gateway itself. Once you see the “✔ APISIX is ready!” message, we can configure an upstream, plugin and route via APISIX's Admin API that proxies requests to GraphQL API.

Create an Upstream

Next, we will create an Upstream object to register our Countries GrapghQL API in the API Gateway:

curl "http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/upstreams/1" -X PUT -d '
{ "name": "GraphQL API upstream", "desc": "Register Countries GraphQL API as the upstream", "type": "roundrobin", "scheme": "https", "nodes": { "countries.trevorblades.com": 1 }
}'

Create a Plugin Config

Next, a set up new plugin config object. We will use two transformation plugins proxy-rewrite and degraphql to rewrite the host and URI of the request and make a query to GraphQL API respectively.

curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/plugin_configs/1 -X PUT -d ' { "plugins":{ "proxy-rewrite":{ "uri":"/graphql", "host":"countries.trevorblades.com" }, "degraphql":{ "query":"query Country($code: ID!) { country(code: $code) { code capital currency languages { name } } }", "variables":[ "code" ] } }
}'

In the above DeGraphQL plugin configuration, we set two attributes such as query and variables. GraphQL query variables can be defined on the Post request body or URIs in a REST call.

The query we’re executing, in this case, looks like the following and you can replace it with your own:

query ($code: ID!) { country(code: $code) { code capital currency languages { name } }
}

Create a Route with DeGraphQL plugin

This step involves setting up a new route that uses the plugin config, and configuring the route to work with the upstream (by referencing their IDs) we created in the previous steps:

curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/1 -X PUT -d '
{ "name":"GraphQL API route", "desc":"Create a new route in APISIX for the Countries GraphQL API", "uri":"/country-info", "upstream_id":"1", "plugin_config_id":1
}'

Test DeGraphQL plugin activation

Now let’s test this new setup with the following curl command.

curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/country-info -X POST -d \
'{ "code": "EE"
}'

We will get response from APISIX:

{ "data": { "country": { "code": "EE", "capital": "Tallinn", "currency": "EUR", "languages": [ { "name": "Estonian" } ] } }
}

The same code variable can also be provided as GET arguments:

curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/country-info?code=EE

Response Transformation

With the help of Apisix's response-rewrite plugin, it is possible to transform GraphQL responses before they are returned to the client. Let's use this plugin to remove the currency key and value from the response JSON to show only everything else. To do so, we need to add the response-rewrite plugin to the existing plugin configuration.

curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/plugin_configs/1 -X PATCH -d ' { "plugins":{ "response-rewrite": { "filters":[ { "regex":"(?:\"currency\":\")(.*?)(?:\")", "scope":"once", "replace":"" } ], "vars":[ [ "status", "==", 200 ] ] } }
}'

After this plugin is installed, you can make a request to /country-info once again and see the transformed response:

{ "data": { "country": { "code": "EE", "capital": "Tallinn", "languages": [ { "name": "Estonian" } ] } }
}

Summary

Overall, the DeGraphQL plugin is an essential tool for any developer building a GraphQL API with APISIX. It has powerful features and easy-to-use configuration making it a breeze to integrate into your existing API gateway, while it supports GraphQL-specific functionality ensuring that your API is performant, reliable, and scalable.

Related resources

  • What is GraphQL?.

  • Apache APISIX DeGraphQL Plugin

Recommended content

🐦 Follow us on Twitter
📝 Find us on Slack
💁 How to contribute page

About the author

Visit my personal blog: www.iambobur.com
item.author: bobur

item.title: 哈希图 | Hashmaps
item.description:
穆罕默德乌斯曼

发表于 3 月 22 日

哈希图，也称为哈希表或字典，是计算机科学中用于高效存储和检索键值对的基本数据结构。 在哈希图中，每个键通过哈希函数映射到特定值，该函数将键转换为数字索引，用于在称为存储桶的类似数组的数据结构中存储和检索关联值。

将键值对添加到 hashmap 的过程包括以下步骤：

  • 散列函数将密钥作为输入并生成散列码，该散列码是一个整数值，以更紧凑和标准化的形式表示密钥。
  • 然后使用散列码计算类似数组的存储桶结构的索引，其中可以存储与键关联的值。
  • 如果已经有一个值存储在计算索引中，则发生了冲突，并且使用冲突解决策略来处理冲突并将新值存储在不同的桶中。

  • 如果计算索引处没有存储值，则新的键值对将存储在该索引处的桶中。
    从哈希图中检索值时，过程类似：

  • 哈希函数应用于密钥以计算哈希码。

  • 散列码用于计算应该存储值的桶结构中的索引。

  • 返回计算索引处的值，或者如果该索引处没有存储值，则该键不在哈希图中。

  • 与其他用于存储键值对的数据结构相比，哈希图具有多项优势。 首先，它们为访问和检索值提供了快速的平均情况性能，两个操作的时间复杂度均为 O(1)。 其次，它们可以处理大量键值对而不需要大量内存。 最后，散列图是动态数据结构，可以根据需要调整大小和重新散列以适应键值对数量的变化。

    执行

class HashTable: def __init__(self, size): self.size = size self.hash_table = self.create_buckets() def create_buckets(self): return [[] for _ in range(self.size)] def set_val(self, key, val): hashed_key = hash(key) % self.size bucket = self.hash_table[hashed_key] found_key = False for index, record in enumerate(bucket): record_key, record_val = record if record_key == key: found_key = True break if found_key: bucket[index] = (key, val) else: bucket.append((key, val)) def get_val(self, key): hashed_key = hash(key) % self.size bucket = self.hash_table[hashed_key] found_key = False for index, record in enumerate(bucket): record_key, record_val = record if record_key == key: found_key = True break if found_key: return record_val else: return "No record found" def delete_val(self, key): hashed_key = hash(key) % self.size bucket = self.hash_table[hashed_key] found_key = False for index, record in enumerate(bucket): record_key, record_val = record if record_key == key: found_key = True break if found_key: bucket.pop(index) return def __str__(self): return "".join(str(item) for item in self.hash_table)

缺点：
然而，哈希图也有一些缺点。 最重要的问题是发生冲突的可能性，如果处理不当，可能会降低数据结构的性能。 此外，hashmaps 可能容易受到某些类型的攻击，例如 hash collision attacks，它可以用来用故意制作的密钥来淹没 bucket 结构。

为了解决这些问题，哈希图使用各种技术来解决冲突，例如链接（其中多个值存储在与链表相同的桶中）或开放寻址（其中探测额外的桶直到找到空桶）。 此外，散列函数可以设计为最小化冲突的可能性或抵抗冲突攻击。

解除暂停后，codeninjausman 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codeninjausman 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 codeninjausman 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 codeninjausman：

降低 codeninjausman 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codeninjausman 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Muhammad Usman

Posted on Mar 22

   

Hashmaps, also known as hash tables or dictionaries, are a fundamental data structure in computer science used to efficiently store and retrieve key-value pairs. In a hashmap, each key is mapped to a specific value through a hash function, which converts the key into a numeric index that is used to store and retrieve the associated value in an array-like data structure called a bucket.

The process of adding a key-value pair to a hashmap involves the following steps:

  • The hash function takes the key as input and produces a hash code, which is an integer value that represents the key in a more compact and standardized form.
  • The hash code is then used to compute an index into the array-like bucket structure, where the value associated with the key can be stored.
  • If there is already a value stored at the computed index, a collision has occurred, and a collision resolution strategy is used to handle the collision and store the new value in a different bucket.

  • If there is no value stored at the computed index, the new key-value pair is stored in the bucket at that index.
    When retrieving a value from a hashmap, the process is similar:

  • The hash function is applied to the key to compute the hash code.

  • The hash code is used to compute the index into the bucket structure where the value should be stored.

  • The value at the computed index is returned, or if there is no value stored at that index, the key is not in the hashmap.

  • Hashmaps have several advantages over other data structures for storing key-value pairs. First, they offer fast average-case performance for accessing and retrieving values, with a time complexity of O(1) for both operations. Second, they can handle a large number of key-value pairs without requiring significant amounts of memory. Finally, hashmaps are dynamic data structures that can be resized and rehashed as needed to accommodate changes in the number of key-value pairs.

    Implementation 

class HashTable:
    def __init__(self, size):
        self.size = size
        self.hash_table = self.create_buckets()

    def create_buckets(self):
        return [[] for _ in range(self.size)]

    def set_val(self, key, val):
        hashed_key = hash(key) % self.size
        bucket = self.hash_table[hashed_key]
        found_key = False
        for index, record in enumerate(bucket):
            record_key, record_val = record
            if record_key == key:
                found_key = True
                break
        if found_key:
            bucket[index] = (key, val)
        else:
            bucket.append((key, val))

    def get_val(self, key):
        hashed_key = hash(key) % self.size
        bucket = self.hash_table[hashed_key]
        found_key = False
        for index, record in enumerate(bucket):
            record_key, record_val = record
            if record_key == key:
                found_key = True
                break
        if found_key:
            return record_val
        else:
            return "No record found"

    def delete_val(self, key):
        hashed_key = hash(key) % self.size
        bucket = self.hash_table[hashed_key]
        found_key = False
        for index, record in enumerate(bucket):
            record_key, record_val = record
            if record_key == key:
                found_key = True
                break
        if found_key:
            bucket.pop(index)
        return

    def __str__(self):
        return "".join(str(item) for item in self.hash_table)

Disadvantages:
However, hashmaps also have some disadvantages. The most significant issue is the possibility of collisions, which can degrade the performance of the data structure if not handled properly. Additionally, hashmaps can be vulnerable to certain types of attacks, such as hash collision attacks, which can be used to overwhelm the bucket structure with intentionally crafted keys.

To address these issues, hashmaps use various techniques for collision resolution, such as chaining (where multiple values are stored in the same bucket as a linked list) or open addressing (where additional buckets are probed until an empty bucket is found). Additionally, hash functions can be designed to minimize the likelihood of collisions or to be resistant to collision attacks.

item.title: 将数据嵌入到 React/JSX 元素中 | Embedding Data Into React/JSX Elements
item.description:
场景

让我们看一个非常基本的函数：

 const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`}> {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

此函数只是构建特定行的表格单元格。 它在我的paintmap.studio应用程序中用于构建“颜色图”。 它会生成一个巨大的网格（表格），为网格中的每个块向我显示我的哪幅画与该特定块最匹配。

构建此功能后，我决定向每个单元格添加一个onClick事件。 这个想法是，当您单击任何给定的单元格时，它会突出显示网格中包含与您刚刚单击的单元格相同颜色的每个单元格。

无论何时单击单元格， onClick事件处理程序都需要了解您选择的颜色。 换句话说，您需要将颜色传递给onClick事件处理程序。 一遍又一遍，我看到看起来像这样的代码来完成那种功能：

 const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex)} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

上面的代码不是“错误的”。 它会将paintIndex传递给事件处理程序。 但这并不是真正的...最佳。 出现的低效率是，对于每个单独的表格单元格，我们正在创建一个全新的函数定义。 这就是paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex)所做的。 它启动了一个全新的功能。 如果你有一个大表，那就是很多函数定义。 这些函数不仅需要在组件的初始渲染时重新定义，而且每当重新调用该组件时也需要重新定义。

理想情况下，您应该为事件处理程序定义一个静态函数。 这看起来像这样：

 const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

在上面的代码中， handleCellClick已经在这个函数之外定义了。 因此，React 不需要在我们每次渲染表格单元格时都重新构建一个全新的函数定义。 不幸的是，这也不完全有效。 因为现在，每次用户单击表格单元格时，事件处理程序都不知道单击了哪个特定单元格。 所以它不知道要突出显示哪个paintIndex 。

同样，我通常看到这种实现方式，即使是在构建良好的代码库中，也是使用paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex)方法。 但正如我已经指出的那样，这是低效的。

因此，让我们看一下解决此问题的几种方法。

包装组件

一种方法是为我的表格单元格创建一个包装器组件。 那看起来像这样：

 const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <MyTableCell key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} paintIndex={paintIndex} > {paintIndex} </MyTableCell> ); }) }

在这种情况下，我们不再使用<td>的基本 HTML 属性。 相反，有一个自定义组件，它将接受paintIndex作为道具，然后大概使用该道具来构建事件处理程序。 使用这种方法，我们不必将paintIndex值传递给handleCellClick事件处理程序，因为我们可以简单地从 prop 中引用它。 这个解决方案有很多值得喜欢的地方，因为它与 React 的惯用方法一致。 理想情况下，您甚至可以记忆MyTableCell组件，这样它就不会在我们每次构建使用相同颜色的表格单元格时重新挂载（和重新渲染）。

但是，这种方法也可能感觉有点繁琐，因为我们正在制作另一个组件纯粹是为了使那个onClick事件更有效率。 此外，如果handleCellClick事件处理程序需要执行影响调用组件中该状态的其他逻辑，则生成的代码可能会有点“繁重”。

有时您希望在调用组件内部处理该事件处理程序的逻辑。 幸运的是，还有其他方法可以做到这一点。

例如，您可以使用longdesc属性将paintIndex直接嵌入 HTML 元素本身。 不幸的是， longdesc仅在<frame> 、 <iframe>和<img>元素中“被允许”。

诚然，浏览器对 HTML 属性的使用非常宽容。 因此，如果您开始将longdesc属性放在各种“非法”HTML 元素上，它实际上不会破坏任何东西。 浏览器基本上只会忽略非惯用属性。 事实上，您甚至可以将自己的自定义属性添加到 HTML 元素中。

尽管如此，避免将一堆不允许的或完全自定义的属性填充到您的 HTML 元素中通常是一种很好的做法。 但我们有更多的选择。 更多“标准”选项。

如果您想找到一个几乎可以放在任何元素上的属性，首先要做的是查看（几乎）任何元素中允许的属性。 它们如下：

  • id
  • class
  • style
  • title
  • dir
  • 这些属性的好处在于，您几乎可以在 HTML正文的任何​​地方使用它们，几乎可以在任何HTML 元素上使用它们，而且您不必担心它们是否“被允许”。 例如，您可以将title属性放在<div>上，或将dir属性放在<td>上。 这都是“可接受的”——也就是说，按照 HTML 标准。

    因此，如果您想使用其中一个属性将数据“传递”到事件处理程序中，最好的选择是什么？

    title

    首先，尽管使用title之类的东西很诱人，但我不推荐这样做。 同样，您应该避免使用dir 、 lang或xml:lang属性。 弄乱这些属性可能会提高网站对国际用户（即使用不同语言的网站的用户）的实用性。 所以，请不要管那些。

    style

    此外，您可以尝试将“自定义”数据填充到style属性中。 但是恕我直言，这看起来会很复杂。 理论上，您可以像这样定义自定义样式属性：

     <td style={{paintIndex: `${paintIndex}`}}>

    然后您可以尝试在事件处理程序中捕获元素时读取元素上的这个虚构样式属性。 但是......我不推荐这种方法。 一点也不。 首先，如果您在元素上设置了任何合法的style属性，您将最终得到真实属性和虚构属性的大杂烩。 其次，没有理由以如此冗长的格式嵌入数据。 使用我们提供的其他选项，您可以做得更“干净”。

    id

    这就是它的样子：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td id={paintIndex} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    在这里，我们使用paintIndex作为id 。 我们为什么要这样做？ 因为这样我们就可以创建一个如下所示的事件处理程序：

     const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(event.target.id); }

    这是有效的，因为传递给事件处理程序的合成事件将嵌入其中的单击元素的id 。 这允许我们在每个表格单元格上使用通用事件处理程序，同时仍然允许事件处理程序准确了解单击了哪个paintIndex 。

    这仍然有一些缺点。 首先， id应该是唯一的。 在上面的示例中，给定的paintIndex可能出现在单个表格单元格中 - 或者出现在数百个表格单元格中。 如果我们简单地使用paintIndex值作为id ，我们最终会得到许多具有相同id值的表格单元格。 值得庆幸的是，我们也可以解决这个问题。 请注意，我们的表格单元格具有key 。 键必须是唯一的。 在这种情况下，我通过使用行/单元格计数来构建密钥来解决该问题。 因为没有两个单元格具有相同的行/单元格编号组合。 我们可以将相同的格式添加到我们的id 。 看起来像这样：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td id={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}-${paintIndex}`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    现在，我们的任何单元格上都不会出现重复的id 。 但是我们仍然将paintIndex值嵌入到该id中。 那么我们如何提取事件处理程序中的值呢？ 看起来像这样：

     const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { const paintIndex = event.target.id.split('-').pop(); uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(paintIndex); }

    由于我们编写了这段代码，并且由于我们确定了id的命名约定，我们还知道paintIndex值将是由-分隔的值字符串中的最后一个值。 如果我们split('-')该字符串，然后pop()其末尾的最后一个值，我们知道我们正在获取paintIndex值。

    class

    如果您熟悉 jQuery UI，您可能已经看到许多实例，其中class被用作一种实际上并不驱动 CSS 样式的“开关”。 相反，它告诉 JavaScript 代码要做什么。

    当然，在 JSX 中我们不使用class 。 我们使用className 。 所以这个解决方案看起来像这样：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`${paintIndex}`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    事件处理程序如下所示：

     const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(event.target.className); }

    正如我们之前从事件对象的id字段中获取paintIndex一样，我们现在从className中获取它。 如果您在className属性中也有“真正的”CSS 类，这将如何工作？ 那看起来像这样：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`cell ${paintIndex}`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    事件处理程序看起来像这样：'

     const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { const paintIndex = event.target.className.split(' ').pop(); uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(paintIndex); }

    但我们还将paintIndex值嵌入到className属性中，并通过在空白处拆分字符串来提取它。

    公平地说，这种方法可能感觉有点……“脆弱”。 因为它取决于paintIndex值是空格分隔的className字符串中的最后一个值。 如果另一个开发人员进来并在className字段的末尾添加了另一个 CSS 类，如下所示：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`cell ${paintIndex} anotherCSSClass`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    逻辑会崩溃。 因为事件处理程序会从字符串的末尾抓取anotherCSSClass - 并尝试将其视为paintIndex 。 如果你想让它更健壮一点，你可以把逻辑改成这样：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`cell paintIndex-${paintIndex} anotherCSSClass`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    然后像这样更新事件处理程序：

     const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { const paintIndex = event.target.className .split(' ') .find(className => className.includes('paintIndex-')) .split('-') .pop(); uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(paintIndex); }

    通过这种方式，为paintIndex提取的值不依赖于是否是空格分隔字符串中的最后一项。 它可以存在于className属性内的任何位置，只要它前面加上“paintIndex-”。

    你为什么要关心？

    坦率地说，在小型应用程序中，拥有这样的东西并不完全是联邦犯罪：

     const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex)} > {paintIndex} </td> ); }) }

    通过在onClick属性中定义新的函数定义而导致的性能“命中”是......最小的。 在某些情况下，尝试“修复”它可以理解为“微优化”。 但我确实相信，养成尽可能避免这些的习惯是一种可靠的做法。

    当事件处理程序不需要将信息从被点击的元素传递给它时，将箭头函数排除在事件属性之外是一个明智的选择。 但是当它确实需要特定于元素的信息时，我经常看到人们盲目地求助于将箭头函数放入其属性中的简单方法。 但是有很多方法可以避免这种情况——而且它们几乎不需要额外的努力。

    凯瑟琳·格雷森·南茨 - 3 月 17 日

    一旦暂停，bytebodger 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

    解除暂停后，bytebodger 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，bytebodger 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 bytebodger 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 bytebodger：

    降低 bytebodger 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

取消标记 bytebodger 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

The Scenario

Let's look at a really basic function:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`}> {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

This function simply builds a particular row of table cells. It's used in my paintmap.studio app to build a "color map". It generates a giant grid (table) that shows me, for every block in the grid, which one of my paints most-closely matches that particular block.

Once this feature was built, I decided that I wanted to add an onClick event to each cell. The idea is that, when you click on any given cell, it then highlights every cell in the grid that contains the same color as the one you've just clicked upon.

Whenever you click on a cell, the onClick event handler needs to understand which color you've chosen. In other words, you need to pass the color into the onClick event handler. Over and over again, I see code that looks like this to accomplish that kinda functionality:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex)} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

The code above isn't "wrong". It will pass the paintIndex to the event handler. But it isn't really... optimal. The inefficiency that arises is that, for every single table cell, we're creating a brand new function definition. That's what paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex) does. It spins up an entirely new function. If you have a large table, that's a lotta function definitions. And those functions need to be redefined not just on the component's initial render, but whenever this component is re-invoked.

Ideally, you'd have a static function definition for the event handler. That would look something like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

In the above code, the handleCellClick has already been defined outside this function. So React doesn't need to rebuild a brand new function definition every single time that we render a table cell. Unfortunately, this doesn't entirely work either. Because now, every time the user clicks on a table cell, the event handler will have no idea which particular cell was clicked. So it won't know which paintIndex to highlight.

Again, the way I normally see this implemented, even in well-built codebases, is to use the paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex) approach. But as I've already pointed out, this is inefficient.

So let's look at a couple ways to remedy this.

Wrapper Components

One approach is to create a wrapper component for my table cells. That would look like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <MyTableCell key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} paintIndex={paintIndex} > {paintIndex} </MyTableCell>
 ); })
}

In this scenario, we're no longer using the base HTML attribute of <td>. Instead, there's a custom component, that will accept paintIndex as a prop, and then presumably use that prop to build the event handler. MyTableCell would look something like this:

const MyTableCell = paintIndex => { const handleCellClick = () => { // event handler logic using paintIndex } return ( <td onClick={handleCellClick}> {paintIndex} </td>
 )
}

Using this approach, we don't have to pass the paintIndex value into the handleCellClick event handler, because we can simply reference it from the prop. There's much to like in this solution because it's consistent with an idiomatic approach to React. Ideally, you'd even memoize the MyTableCell component so it doesn't get remounted (and re rendered) every time we build a table cell that uses the same paint color.

However, this approach can also feel a bit onerous because we're cranking out another component purely for the sake of making that one onClick event more efficient. Also, if the handleCellClick event handler needs to do other logic that impacts that state in the calling component, the resulting code can get a bit "heavy".

Sometimes you want the logic for that event handler to be handled right inside the calling component. Luckily, there are other ways to do this.

HTML Attributes

HTML affords us a lot of freedom to "stuff" data where it's needed. For example, you could use the longdesc attribute to embed the paintIndex right into the HTML element itself. Unfortunately, longdesc is only "allowed" in <frame>, <iframe>, and <img> elements.

Granted, browsers are tremendously forgiving about the usage of HTML attributes. So if you were to start putting longdesc attributes on all sorts of "illegal" HTML elements, it really won't break anything. The browser will basically just ignore the non-idiomatic attributes. In fact, you can even add your own custom attributes to HTML elements.

Nevertheless, it's usually good practice to avoid stuffing a buncha non-allowed or completely-custom attributes into your HTML elements. But we have more options. More "standard" options.

(Near) Universal HTML Attributes

If you wanna find an attribute that you can put on pretty much any element, the first things is to look at the attributes that are allowed in (almost) any elements. They are as follows:

  • id
  • class
  • style
  • title
  • dir
  • lang / xml:lang

The nice thing about these attributes is that you can pretty much use them anywhere within the body of your HTML, on pretty much any HTML element, and you don't have to worry about whether they're "allowed". You can, for example, put a title attribute on a <div>, or a dir attribute on a <td>. It's all "acceptable" - by HTML standards, that is.

So if you wanted to use one of these attributes to "pass" data into an event handler, what would be the best choice?

title

First of all, as tempting as it may be to use something like title, I would not recommend this. title is used by screen readers and you're gonna jack up the accessibility of your site if you stuff a bunch of programmatic data into that attribute - data that should not be read by a screen reader.

dir, lang, xml:lang

Similarly, you should avoid appropriating the dir, lang, or xml:lang attributes. Messing with these attributes could jack up the utility of the site for international users (i.e., those who are using your site with a different language). So please, leave those alone as well.

style

Also, you could try to cram "custom" data into a style attribute. But IMHO, that's gonna come out looking convoluted. In theory, you could define a custom style property like this:

<td style={{paintIndex: `${paintIndex}`}}>

Then you could try to read this made-up style property on the element when it's captured in the event handler. But... I don't recommend such an approach. Not at all. First, if you have any legitimate style properties set on the element, you're gonna end up with a mishmash of real and made-up properties. Second, there's no reason to embed data in such a verbose format. You can do it much "cleaner" with the other options at our disposal.

id

The id attribute can be a great place to "embed" data. Here's what that would look like:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td id={paintIndex} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

Here, we're using the paintIndex as the id. Why would we do this? Because then we can create an event handler that looks like this:

const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(event.target.id);
}

This works because the synthetic event that's passed to the event handler will have the id of the clicked element embedded within it. This allows us to use a generic event handler on each table cell, while still allowing the event handler to understand exactly which paintIndex was clicked upon.

This can still have some drawbacks. First of all, ids are supposed to be unique. In the example above, a given paintIndex may be present in a single table cell - or in hundreds of them. And if we simply use the paintIndex value as the id, we'll end up with many table cells that have identical id values. (To be clear, having duplicate ids won't break your HTML display. But in some scenarios it can break your JavaScript logic.)

Thankfully, we can fix that, too. Notice that our table cells have key values. And keys must be unique. In this scenario, I addressed that problem by using the row/cell counts to build the key. Because no two cells will have the same combination of row/cell numbers. We can add the same format to our id. That looks like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td id={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}-${paintIndex}`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

Now, there will never be a duplicate id on any of our cells. But we still have the paintIndex value embedded into that id. So how do we extract the value in our event handler? That looks like this:

const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { const paintIndex = event.target.id.split('-').pop(); uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(paintIndex);
}

Since we wrote this code, and since we determined the naming convention for the id, we also know that the paintIndex value will be the last value in a string of values that are delimited by -. If we split('-') that string and then pop() the last value off the end of it, we know that we're getting the paintIndex value.

class

class is also a great place to "embed" data - even if it doesn't map to any CSS class that's available to the script. If you're familiar with jQuery UI, you've probably seen many instances where class is used as a type of "switch" that doesn't actually drive CSS styles. Instead, it tells the JavaScript code what to do.

Of course, in JSX we don't use class. We use className. So that solution would look like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`${paintIndex}`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

And the event handler looks like this:

const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(event.target.className);
}

Just as we previously grabbed paintIndex from the event object's id field, we're now grabbing it from className. How would this work if you also had "real" CSS classes in the className property? That would look like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`cell ${paintIndex}`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

And the event handler would look like this:'

const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { const paintIndex = event.target.className.split(' ').pop(); uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(paintIndex);
}

The "cell" class on the <td> is a "real" class - meaning that it maps to predefined CSS properties. But we also embedded the paintIndex value into the className property and we extracted it by splitting the string on empty spaces.

To be fair, this approach may feel a bit... "brittle". Because it depends upon the paintIndex value being the last value in the space-delimited className string. If another developer came in and added another CSS class to the end of the className field, like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`cell ${paintIndex} anotherCSSClass`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

The logic would break. Because the event handler would grab anotherCSSClass off the end of the string - and try to treat it like it's the paintIndex. If you'd like to make it a bit more robust, you can change the logic to something like this:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td className={`cell paintIndex-${paintIndex} anotherCSSClass`} key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={handleCellClick} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

And then update the event handler like this:

const handleCellClick = (event = {}) => { const paintIndex = event.target.className .split(' ') .find(className => className.includes('paintIndex-')) .split('-') .pop(); uiState.toggleHighlightedColor(paintIndex);
}

By doing it this way, the value that's extracted for paintIndex isn't dependent upon being the last item in the space-delimited string. It can exist anywhere inside the className property, as long as it's prepended with "paintIndex-".

Why Should You Care?

To be frank, in small apps, having something like this isn't exactly a federal crime:

const getTableCells = (cells = [rgbModel], rowIndex = -1) => { return cells.map((cell, cellIndex) => { const paintIndex = colors.findIndex(color => color.name === cell.name); return ( <td key={`cell-${rowIndex}-${cellIndex}`} onClick={paintIndex => handleCellClick(paintIndex)} > {paintIndex} </td>
 ); })
}

The performance "hit" you incur by defining a new function definition inside the onClick property is... minimal. In some cases, trying to "fix" it could be understandably defined as a "micro-optimization". But I do believe it's a solid practice to to get in the habit of avoiding these whenever possible.

When the event handler doesn't need to have information passed into it from the clicked element, it's a no-brainer to keep arrow functions out of your event properties. But when it does require element-specific info, too often I see people blindly fall back on the easy method of dropping arrow functions into their properties. But there are many ways to avoid this - and they require little additional effort.

Kathryn Grayson Nanz - Mar 17

item.title: 使用模因效果 | useMemeffect
item.description:
戈兰穆斯塔法

发表于 3 月 22 日

如果你想避免在只有一些依赖项发生变化时不必要地重新渲染你的组件，你可以使用useMemo挂钩来记住从依赖项派生的值。 这样，只有当它们所依赖的依赖项发生变化时，才会重新计算这些值。

在上面的示例中，我们使用 useMemo 根据依赖关系计算两个派生值。 然后，我们将这些派生值作为依赖项传递给 useEffect 挂钩。 这意味着 useEffect 挂钩只会在派生值实际发生变化时重新运行，而不是在任何原始依赖项发生变化但派生值保持不变时重新运行。

通过以这种方式使用 useMemo，您可以避免不必要的组件重新渲染，并确保它仅在实际需要时才重新渲染。

一旦暂停，golam_mostafa 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，golam_mostafa 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，golam_mostafa 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 golam_mostafa 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 golam_mostafa：

降低 golam_mostafa 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 golam_mostafa 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Golam_Mostafa

Posted on Mar 22

   

If you want to avoid unnecessary re-renders of your component when only some of the dependencies change, you can use the useMemo hook to memoize the values that are derived from the dependencies. This way, the values will only be re-computed when the dependencies they depend on change.

In the above example, we're using useMemo to compute two derived values based on the dependencies. We then pass these derived values as dependencies to the useEffect hook. This means that the useEffect hook will only re-run when the derived values actually change, not when any of the original dependencies change but the derived values remain the same.

By using useMemo in this way, you can avoid unnecessary re-renders of your component and ensure that it only re-renders when it actually needs to.

item.title: 软件工程 | Software Engineering
item.description:
发表于 3 月 22 日

软件工程是一个不断发展的领域，在当今技术驱动的世界中处于创新的前沿。 它是一门将计算机科学原理与工程实践相结合的学科，旨在设计、开发和维护满足用户和企业需求的高质量软件系统。

软件工程对我们日常生活的影响怎么强调都不为过。 从我们在智能手机上使用的应用程序到运行我们金融机构的复杂系统，软件工程已经改变了我们与技术以及彼此交互的方式。

然而，软件工程并非没有挑战。 随着软件系统复杂性的增加，对能够有效设计、开发和维护这些系统的熟练软件工程师的需求也在增加。 对熟练软件工程师的需求持续上升，该职业已成为科技行业最抢手的职业之一。

要成为一名成功的软件工程师，必须具备技术技能、解决问题的能力和创造力的结合。 软件工程师必须精通编程语言、软件开发工具和开发方法。 他们还必须能够与团队的其他成员协作，并向非技术利益相关者传达复杂的技术概念。

软件工程在当今世界的重要性怎么强调都不为过。 软件工程师是创新技术发展背后的推动力，这些技术正在改变我们的生活、工作和娱乐方式。 随着该领域的不断发展，我们必须继续投资于未来软件工程师的教育和培训，以确保我们拥有应对未来挑战所需的人才和技能。

总之，软件工程是塑造未来技术和我们周围世界的强大力量。 这是一门需要技术专长、解决问题的能力和创造力相结合的学科，并为创新和发展提供了无限的机会。 无论您是经验丰富的软件工程师，还是刚刚开始您在该领域的旅程，成为这个充满活力和回报丰厚的职业的一员，再没有比现在更激动人心的时刻了。

一旦暂停，profosborn 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，profosborn 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，profosborn 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 profosborn 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 profosborn：

降低 profosborn 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 profosborn 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

EDUDZI WORLASI OSBORN

Posted on Mar 22

   

Software engineering is an ever-evolving field that is at the forefront of innovation in today's technology-driven world. It is a discipline that combines computer science principles with engineering practices to design, develop, and maintain high-quality software systems that meet the needs of users and businesses.

The impact of software engineering on our daily lives cannot be overstated. From the applications we use on our smartphones to the complex systems that run our financial institutions, software engineering has transformed the way we interact with technology and each other.

However, software engineering is not without its challenges. As the complexity of software systems increases, so too does the need for skilled software engineers who can design, develop, and maintain these systems effectively. The demand for skilled software engineers continues to rise, and the profession has become one of the most sought-after in the tech industry.

To be a successful software engineer, one must possess a combination of technical skills, problem-solving abilities, and creativity. Software engineers must be proficient in programming languages, software development tools, and development methodologies. They must also be able to work collaboratively with other members of their team and communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

The importance of software engineering in today's world cannot be overstated. Software engineers are the driving force behind the development of innovative technologies that are transforming the way we live, work, and play. As the field continues to evolve, it is essential that we continue to invest in the education and training of future software engineers to ensure that we have the talent and skills needed to meet the challenges of the future.

In conclusion, software engineering is a powerful force that is shaping the future of technology and the world around us. It is a discipline that demands a combination of technical expertise, problem-solving skills, and creativity, and offers endless opportunities for innovation and growth. Whether you are a seasoned software engineer or just starting your journey in the field, there has never been a more exciting time to be a part of this dynamic and rewarding profession.

item.title: 内容写作机构如何帮助提高您的网站流量和参与度 | How a Content Writing Agency Can Help Boost Your Website Traffic and Engagement
item.description:
在当今的数字世界中，拥有强大的在线形象对于任何企业的成功都是必不可少的。 一个设计精良、内容优质的网站是吸引访问者、吸引他们并将他们转化为客户的关键。 这就是内容写作机构的用武之地。专业的内容写作机构可以通过多种方式帮助提高您的网站流量和参与度。

创造高质量的内容
内容写作机构可以创建既信息丰富又引人入胜的高质量内容。 此类内容将吸引访问者访问您的网站，并使他们停留更长时间。 当访问者在您的网站上找到有价值且信息丰富的内容时，他们更有可能在社交媒体上分享这些内容，这有助于为您的网站带来更多流量。

搜索引擎优化
内容写作机构可以通过搜索引擎优化 (SEO)帮助提高您网站的搜索引擎排名。 当您的网站出现在搜索引擎结果页面的顶部时，它将为您的网站带来更多流量。

一致的发布时间表
内容写作机构可以帮助您保持一致的发布时间表。 这意味着定期发布内容，这将帮助您建立忠实的访问者群，这些访问者会一次又一次地回到您的网站。 这种一致性还表明您的企业积极、参与并投资于为您的受众提供有价值的内容。

创建不同类型的内容
内容写作机构可以创建不同类型的内容，例如博客文章、信息图表、白皮书等。 通过创建不同类型的内容，您可以迎合对信息消费方式有不同偏好的不同受众。 这将帮助您覆盖更广泛的受众并保持他们的参与度。

紧跟行业趋势
内容写作机构可以跟上最新的行业趋势，并将其融入您网站的内容中。 这将使您的网站保持相关性和最新性，并帮助您吸引正在寻找您所在行业最新和最重要信息的访问者。

结合视觉效果
内容编写机构可以将图像和视频等视觉效果合并到您网站的内容中。 视觉效果有助于分解长文本块，使您的内容更具吸引力，并改善网站的整体美感。

参与社交媒体内容
内容写作机构还可以帮助您创建引人入胜的社交媒体内容，将流量带回您的网站。 通过创建链接回您网站内容的社交媒体帖子，您可以覆盖更广泛的受众并为您的网站带来更多流量。

总之，内容写作机构可以帮助您提高网站流量和参与度，从而成为您企业的宝贵资产。 通过创建高质量的内容、针对搜索引擎进行优化、保持一致的发布时间表、创建不同类型的内容、跟上行业趋势、整合视觉效果以及创建引人入胜的社交媒体内容，内容写作机构可以帮助您吸引更多访问者，让他们参与进来，并最终将他们转化为客户。 如果您希望提高网站的访问量和参与度，请考虑与内容编写机构合作。



原文:

In today’s digital world, having a strong online presence is essential for the success of any business. A well-designed website with high-quality content is key to attracting visitors, engaging them, and converting them into customers. This is where a content writing agency comes in. A professional content writing agency can help boost your website traffic and engagement in several ways.

Creating high-quality content
A content writing agency can create high-quality content that is both informative and engaging. Such content will attract visitors to your website, and keep them there for longer periods. When visitors find valuable and informative content on your website, they are more likely to share it on social media, which can help drive even more traffic to your website.

Search engine optimization
A content writing agency can help improve your website’s search engine rankings through search engine optimization (SEO). SEO involves using relevant keywords and phrases that users are likely to search for on search engines. When your website appears at the top of search engine results pages, it will drive more traffic to your website.

Consistent publishing schedule
A content writing agency can help you maintain a consistent publishing schedule. This means publishing content on a regular basis, which will help you build a loyal following of visitors who come back to your website time and time again. This consistency also shows that your business is active, engaged, and invested in providing valuable content to your audience.

Creating different types of content
A content writing agency can create different types of content, such as blog posts, infographics, white papers, and more. By creating different types of content, you can cater to different audiences with varying preferences for how they consume information. This will help you reach a wider audience and keep them engaged.

Keeping up with industry trends
A content writing agency can keep up with the latest industry trends and incorporate them into your website’s content. This will keep your website relevant and up-to-date, and help you attract visitors who are looking for the latest and greatest information in your industry.

Incorporating visuals
A content writing agency can incorporate visuals, such as images and videos, into your website’s content. Visuals can help break up long blocks of text, make your content more engaging, and improve your website’s overall aesthetic.

Engaging social media content
A content writing agency can also help you create engaging social media content that drives traffic back to your website. By creating social media posts that link back to your website’s content, you can reach a wider audience and drive more traffic to your website.

In conclusion, a content writing agency can be a valuable asset to your business by helping you boost your website traffic and engagement. By creating high-quality content, optimizing for search engines, maintaining a consistent publishing schedule, creating different types of content, keeping up with industry trends, incorporating visuals, and creating engaging social media content, a content writing agency can help you attract more visitors, keep them engaged, and ultimately convert them into customers. If you’re looking to improve your website’s traffic and engagement, consider partnering with a content writing agency.
item.author: bennywrity33

item.title: 记忆化：对速度的需求 | Memoization: The need for speed
item.description:
记忆化是一种编程技术，用于优化频繁执行的函数的性能。 它涉及缓存函数调用的结果，以便可以为具有相同参数的后续调用返回相同的结果，而不是每次都重新计算它们。

记忆化基于这样的想法，即如果多次使用相同的参数调用函数，则每次都会返回相同的结果。 因此，缓存第一个函数调用的结果并为所有具有相同参数的后续调用返回它是有意义的。

记忆化的过程涉及创建一个缓存对象，该对象用于存储函数调用的结果。 当使用一组参数调用函数时，将检查缓存对象以查看这些参数的结果是否已经存在。 如果是，则返回缓存的结果； 否则，执行该函数，并将结果存储在缓存对象中以备将来使用。

记忆对于计算量大或逻辑复杂的函数特别有用。 通过缓存第一次调用的结果，可以快速执行具有相同参数的后续调用，而无需重复相同的计算。

在 Node.js 中，可以使用多种技术实现记忆化。 一种常见的方法是使用 JavaScript 对象来存储缓存的结果。 这是一个简单的记忆功能的例子：

 function memoize(func) { const cache = {}; return function(...args) { const key = JSON.stringify(args); if (cache[key]) { return cache[key]; } const result = func.apply(this, args); cache[key] = result; return result; }; }

该函数将一个函数作为参数并返回一个新函数，该函数用记忆包装了原始函数。 缓存对象被创建为一个空的 JavaScript 对象，并返回一个新函数，该函数将用于执行记忆函数。

使用 JSON.stringify() 方法将这些参数转换为字符串，该方法用作缓存对象的键。 如果缓存中存在key，则返回缓存结果。 如果没有，则使用 apply() 方法执行原始函数，并将结果存储在缓存对象中以备将来使用。

以下是如何使用记忆功能的示例：

 function fibonacci(n) { if (n <= 1) { return n; } return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2); } const memoizedFibonacci = memoize(fibonacci); console.log(memoizedFibonacci(10)); // Output: 55 console.log(memoizedFibonacci(10)); // Output: 55 (cached result)

在此示例中， fibonacci() 函数计算给定数字的斐波那契数列。 然后使用相同的参数调用 memoizedFibonacci() 函数两次，并在第二次调用时从缓存对象返回结果。

记忆化是一种强大的技术，可以极大地提高 Node.js 应用程序的性能。 通过缓存频繁执行的函数的结果，您可以避免重复昂贵的计算并减少整体处理时间。

记忆化是优化执行 SQL 查询的函数性能的有效技术之一。 通过缓存先前查询的结果，可以快速处理具有相同参数的后续调用，而无需重新执行查询。 另一种提高性能的方法是使用物化表，它将查询的结果存储在物理表中，以便后续查询可以直接从表中检索预先计算的结果。 您对物化表主题感兴趣吗？ 我在另一篇文章中讨论过。



原文:

Memoization is a technique used in programming to optimize the performance of frequently executed functions. It involves caching the results of a function call, so that the same results can be returned for subsequent calls with the same arguments, rather than recalculating them each time.

Memoization is based on the idea that if a function is called with the same arguments multiple times, it will return the same result each time. Therefore, it makes sense to cache the result of the first function call and return it for all subsequent calls with the same arguments.

The process of memoization involves creating a cache object, which is used to store the results of function calls. When a function is called with a set of arguments, the cache object is checked to see if the result for those arguments already exists. If it does, the cached result is returned; otherwise, the function is executed, and the result is stored in the cache object for future use.

Memoization can be particularly useful for functions that are computationally expensive or have complex logic. By caching the result of the first call, subsequent calls with the same arguments can be executed quickly and without the need to repeat the same calculations.

In Node.js, memoization can be implemented using a variety of techniques. One common approach is to use a JavaScript object to store the cached results. Here's an example of a simple memoization function:

function memoize(func) {
  const cache = {};
  return function(...args) {
    const key = JSON.stringify(args);
    if (cache[key]) {
      return cache[key];
    }
    const result = func.apply(this, args);
    cache[key] = result;
    return result;
  };
}

This function takes a function as an argument and returns a new function that wraps the original function with memoization. The cache object is created as an empty JavaScript object, and a new function is returned that will be used to execute the memoized function.

The memoized function takes a variable number of arguments using the spread syntax ...args. These arguments are converted into a string using the JSON.stringify() method, which is used as the key for the cache object. If the key exists in the cache, the cached result is returned. If it doesn't, the original function is executed using the apply() method, and the result is stored in the cache object for future use.

Here's an example of how you can use the memoization function:

function fibonacci(n) {
  if (n <= 1) {
    return n;
  }
  return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2);
}

const memoizedFibonacci = memoize(fibonacci);

console.log(memoizedFibonacci(10)); // Output: 55
console.log(memoizedFibonacci(10)); // Output: 55 (cached result)

In this example, the fibonacci() function calculates the Fibonacci sequence for a given number. The memoize() function is used to create a new function memoizedFibonacci that wraps the fibonacci() function with memoization. The memoizedFibonacci() function is then called twice with the same argument, and the result is returned from the cache object on the second call.

Memoization is a powerful technique that can greatly improve the performance of your Node.js applications. By caching the results of frequently executed functions, you can avoid repeating expensive calculations and reduce the overall processing time.

Memoization is one of effective techniques for optimizing the performance of functions that execute SQL queries. By caching the results of previous queries, subsequent calls with the same arguments can be served quickly without having to re-execute the query. Another approach for improving performance is to use materialized tables, which store the results of a query in a physical table, so that subsequent queries can retrieve the pre-calculated results directly from the table. Are you interested in materialized tables topic? I discussed it in another post.
item.author: kerisnarendra

item.title: Rust 实时 OTP 算法 | Rust Realtime OTP Algorithm
item.description:
鸦片版钾

发表于 3 月 22 日

基于时间的一次性密码 (TOTP) 算法在 Rust 中的实现

此 Rust 程序提供基于时间的一次性密码 (TOTP) 算法的实现，该算法用于为多重身份验证系统生成一次性密码。 该算法基于共享秘密和当前时间，并生成每 30 秒更改一次的 6 位数字代码。

实施细节

该算法的核心函数是generate_otp(secret: &str) ，它将共享密钥作为输入并返回一个每 30 秒更改一次的 6 位数字代码。 该函数使用以下步骤生成 OTP：

  1. 使用SystemTime和UNIX_EPOCH结构获取自 Unix 纪元以来的当前时间（以秒为单位）。
  2. 将当前时间除以 30 得到当前时间窗口。
  3. 使用 rand crate 中的thread_rng()函数生成 0 到 999999 之间的随机数。
  4. 将当前时间窗口与随机数异或以产生中间值。
  5. 取中间值 mod 1000000 以生成 6 位 OTP。

用法示例

该程序的main()函数通过在循环中调用generate_otp函数并每 30 秒将生成的 OTP 打印到控制台来演示如何在实践中使用此 OTP 算法。 这模拟了通常用于多因素身份验证系统的 OTP 的实时生成。

 use rand::{Rng, thread_rng}; use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH}; use std::thread; use std::time; fn generate_otp(secret: &str) -> u32 { let now = SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_secs() / 30; let mut rng = thread_rng(); let random_number: u32 = rng.gen_range(0..1000000); let otp = (now as u32 ^ random_number) % 1000000; otp } fn main() { let secret = "xxx_Your_Secret_Here_xxx"; loop { let otp = generate_otp(secret); println!("Your OTP is: {}", otp); thread::sleep(time::Duration::from_secs(30)); } }

安全注意事项

请注意，TOTP 算法的安全性取决于共享秘密的保密性，该秘密应保密，不得与未经授权的用户共享。 此外，该程序应与适当的安全措施结合使用，例如 SSL/TLS 加密和强密码策略，以确保用户帐户和敏感数据的安全。

此外

如果您想获得包含 SSL/TLS 的教程，请通过 twitter @SensoryKopi与我互动或联系我

富兰克林马丁内斯 - 3 月 10 日

一旦被暂停，kopium 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，kopium 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，kopium 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 kopium 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 kopium 的方法：

降低 kopium 的所有帖子的可见性

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging kopium will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning kopium 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Opium ver. K

Posted on Mar 22

   

Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) Algorithm Implementation in Rust

This Rust program provides an implementation of the Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) algorithm, which is used to generate one-time passwords for multi-factor authentication systems. The algorithm is based on a shared secret and the current time, and produces a 6-digit numerical code that changes every 30 seconds.

Implementation Details

The core function of the algorithm is generate_otp(secret: &str), which takes a shared secret as input and returns a 6-digit numerical code that changes every 30 seconds. The function uses the following steps to generate the OTP:

  1. Get the current time in seconds since the Unix epoch using the SystemTime and UNIX_EPOCH structs.
  2. Divide the current time by 30 to get the current time window.
  3. Generate a random number between 0 and 999999 using the thread_rng() function from the rand crate.
  4. XOR the current time window with the random number to produce an intermediate value.
  5. Take the intermediate value mod 1000000 to produce a 6-digit OTP.

Example Usage

The main() function of the program demonstrates how this OTP algorithm can be used in practice, by calling the generate_otp function in a loop and printing the resulting OTP to the console every 30 seconds. This simulates the real-time generation of OTPs that would typically be used in multi-factor authentication systems.

use rand::{Rng, thread_rng};
use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
use std::thread;
use std::time; fn generate_otp(secret: &str) -> u32 { let now = SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_secs() / 30; let mut rng = thread_rng(); let random_number: u32 = rng.gen_range(0..1000000); let otp = (now as u32 ^ random_number) % 1000000; otp
} fn main() { let secret = "xxx_Your_Secret_Here_xxx"; loop { let otp = generate_otp(secret); println!("Your OTP is: {}", otp); thread::sleep(time::Duration::from_secs(30)); }
}

Security Considerations

Note that the security of the TOTP algorithm depends on the secrecy of the shared secret, which should be kept confidential and not shared with unauthorized users. Additionally, the program should be used in conjunction with appropriate security measures, such as SSL/TLS encryption and strong password policies, to ensure the security of user accounts and sensitive data.

Additionally

If you'd like to get a tutorial that includes SSL/TLS please interact or contact me via twitter @SensoryKopi

Franklin Martinez - Mar 10

Victor Tihomirov - Mar 11

Daniel Bemsen Akosu - Mar 8

Once suspended, kopium will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, kopium will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by kopium will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If kopium is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag kopium:

Make all posts by kopium less visible

kopium consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging kopium will restore default visibility to their posts.
